gran_impulso_ambiental_concreto.evidencia: Test 1 (paquete de inversión masiva y coordinada): no se cumple. El documento es un diagnóstico de mercado del instrumento green bond en ALC y cierra con consideraciones para ampliar su uso; no propone un paquete simultáneo de inversiones/políticas a escala de modelo de desarrollo. Test 2 (sectores estratégicos + instrumento): sí — nombra energía renovable, transporte, agricultura/silvicultura, agua y control de contaminación, y el instrumento concreto son los green/climate-aligned bonds (p. ej. US$ 8.4 mil millones emitidos 2014-2017). Test 3 (tres eficiencias): solo la ambiental está desarrollada (proyectos climáticos/bajo carbono); crecimiento e infraestructura aparecen como marco, sin eficiencia schumpeteriana ni keynesiana elaboradas. Lectura: instrumento sectorial/financiero sin lógica de Big Push → Parcial (no Solo aspiracional → No).

citas.cita: The growth of the green bond market in the region could help increase the share of private investment in environmentally-friendly infrastructure and channel the future growth of national savings to green projects that help the region respond to its sustainable development challenges.

A majority of the bonds issued in the region are directed at financing energy and transportation-related projects as well as agricultural and forestry initiatives. Multi-sector initiatives account for 35% of total climate-sensitive bond issuance.

If the region is to meet its demand for infrastructure, for example, it must invest at least US$ 320 billion annually on average (6.2% of GDP) in the coming years according to ECLAC (2014), double the amount invested in the last decade.

articulacion_actores.evidencia: Nombra actores concretos del ámbito ALC (bancos de desarrollo Banobras/NAFIN/BNDES/Bancóldex, fondos de pensión, IFC, BID, BVC, emisores corporativos) y describe transacciones y cooperación técnica puntual (p. ej. estructuración BID-SECO del bono de Bancóldex). Los mecanismos con nombre propio que desarrolla con detalle —Green Bond Principles (ICMA) y Climate Bonds Standard/Certification Scheme— son estándares voluntarios globales de mercado, no un mecanismo de planificación multiactor adoptado para la región. La referencia a la Bolsa Mexicana aparece solo como modelo a imitar en una consideración final (“similar to what the Mexican Stock Exchange has done”), sin describir un proceso institucionalizado de articulación regional. Box 1 (PBoC, NDRC, Green Finance Committee de China) es referente extrarregional y topea en Parcial. No hay consejo/mesa/plan multiactor con nombre propio adoptado en el ámbito ALC → Parcial.

citas.cita: Bring together national market actors with private and institutional investors similar to what the Mexican Stock Exchange has done in Mexico to increase green-financing awareness.

The bond was structured with technical cooperation from the Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) with resources from the Secretariat of State for Economic Affairs of Switzerland (SECO) and certified by the Climate Bonds Initiative. It was the country’s first green bond available through the Colombian Stock Exchange (BVC, Bolsa de Valores de Colombia).
Cooperation between national and local governments and private investors (domestic and foreign), as well as regional and multilateral banks, will prove crucial in this endeavor.

oportunidades_productivas_sostenibles.evidencia: Elemento (a) enumeración de sectores/actividades: sí, de forma extensa — renovables, eficiencia energética, transporte limpio, agua/saneamiento, agricultura y silvicultura sostenibles, biodiversidad, edificios verdes (Tabla 1 GBP) y destinos multi-sector de emisores ALC (Tabla 3: Banobras, Bancóldex, CMPC, etc.). Elemento (b) vínculo desarrollado con empleo/productividad/desigualdad: no. Aparece el nexo infraestructura–crecimiento y, en nota al pie sobre bonos “sustainable”, una mención aislada a “employment, education and healthcare”; la categoría Banobras de infraestructura asequible para segmentos vulnerables no se desarrolla como política de empleo o reducción de desigualdad. Chequeo negativo explícito: se buscaron pasajes que conecten la emisión verde con creación de empleo, productividad o desigualdad con cifra o mecanismo de política — no hay desarrollo más allá de menciones aisladas. Enumeración sin vínculo elaborado → Parcial.

citas.cita: The GBP proposes a list of categories that highlight some of the main types of projects financed by green bonds (Table 1). […] Renewable Energy; Energy Efficiency; Pollution Prevention & Control; […] Transportation; Water and wastewater management; […] Green buildings.
There is a consensus regarding the importance of infrastructure spending in stimulating economic growth and its fundamental role in addressing the region’s development challenges.
These include protecting biodiversity or reducing resource use, or socially positive activities such as employment, education and healthcare.

como_hacerlo_concreto.evidencia: La sección de recomendaciones operativas es IV. Conclusion (pp. 41-42), tras el bloque de “main takeaways” (diagnóstico). El desglose se corre sobre las tres consideraciones numeradas que el texto introduce como “the following considerations should be explored” (más el párrafo inmediato sobre banca multilateral, que opera como cuarto ítem de “cómo”). 3 de 3 ítems numerados pasan el test de concreción (objeto/instrumento identificable; el verbo blando no decide). Tally de la lista numerada: 3 de 3 → Sí.

citas.cita: To continue the region’s solidifying growth in the climate-aligned bond market the following considerations should be explored: i. Develop local markets: promote visibility of local demand for green bonds while establishing a set of local and international guidelines for issuers to follow. Bring together national market actors with private and institutional investors similar to what the Mexican Stock Exchange has done in Mexico to increase green-financing awareness.

ii. Incentivize and regulate: provide a friendly regulatory environment for investors to support green projects, including removing barriers that hinder green bond investment, offering incentives to investors willing to invest in green bond initiatives and enhancing private and public credit channels for green infrastructure.

iii. Increase the role of nontraditional investors: […] Institutional investors, particularly pension and private funds, could help cover the remaining gap and play a more significant role in the market by committing to increase the participation of bonds with a green focus […] in their portfolios.

desglose_items.item: Develop local markets: establish local and international guidelines for issuers; bring together national market actors with private/institutional investors modeled on the Mexican Stock Exchange’s green-financing awareness work.
Incentivize and regulate: remove barriers to green bond investment; offer incentives to green-bond investors; enhance private and public credit channels for green infrastructure.
Increase the role of nontraditional investors: pension and private funds commit to raising the share of green-focus bonds in their portfolios.

como_hacerlo_concreto.tally: 3 de 3 ítems pasan el test de concreción

tipologia.justificacion_breve: El sujeto del informe es el mercado de green bonds como instrumento de financing for development en ALC: cuantifica US$ 8.4 mil millones en climate-aligned bonds (2014-2017), compara emisiones locales e internacionales y discute fondos de pensión, garantías y banca de desarrollo. La tensión que procesa es la brecha de inversión en infraestructura (US$ 320 mil millones anuales / 6.2% del PIB) frente a fuentes tradicionales insuficientes. Lo ambiental entra como destino de los proceeds (energía eólica/solar, transporte, silvicultura, agua) y como ancla Paris/2030 Agenda, no como transformación productiva autónoma.
